title: Armorless Eva Pic Fake or Real
display_title: Armorless Eva Pic Fake or Real
wikitext:
[]: Armorless Eva Pic: Fake or Real?             Quote Post by Mark2000 » Wed Jan 26, 2005 2:33 pm A long time lurker, first time poster here. I finally have something to contribute... I came across this recently via a friend. I haven't seen it on the net on in a magazine before so I don't know where it comes from. Anyone seen it before? It's a pretty bad scan of a pretty awesome looking pic. <link deleted>

[]:             Quote Post by Sanada » Wed Jan 26, 2005 4:54 pm its obviously eva01 without its armour/restraints

[]:             Quote Post by Mark2000 » Wed Jan 26, 2005 4:57 pm I understand that. But is it official art? If so, it is the first time I've seen a completely armorless evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Sanada » Wed Jan 26, 2005 4:59 pm its hard to say youd need to look for it on the gainax site maybe It certainly is a good fake if it is one

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Wed Jan 26, 2005 5:43 pm looks interesting but no private parts is wierd cuz they are somewhat human afterall. ps: plz don't double post

[]:             Quote Post by Man Chong » Wed Jan 26, 2005 9:12 pm ChronoX wrote: looks interesting but no private parts is wierd cuz they are somewhat human afterall. ps: plz don't double post

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Wed Jan 26, 2005 10:48 pm Hi! As an naked eva expert, the head looks more like the MP evas. Maybe it's an official card or something like that, but the artist has never participated on the series. The most official naked eva pictures are the ones from sadamoto's manga, and here it is a little collage of images:

[]:             Quote Post by Trotsky » Wed Jan 26, 2005 11:08 pm Hmmm, donno what makes you an expert since you could not have been part of the production yourself... Nevertheless, I don't know what the Manga has to do will the series. That pic doesnt look anything like eva01. There's no core. There's no mouth, and none of the body marking corespond to anything seen in the series. Looks more like eva in a spiderman outfit.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jan 26, 2005 11:39 pm Trotsky wrote: Hmmm, donno what makes you an expert since you could not have been part of the production yourself...

[]:             Quote Post by Trotsky » Wed Jan 26, 2005 11:41 pm It still doesn't look like anything in the series. The thing at the top of the thread looks way more accurate.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 27, 2005 12:04 am That picture is one of the fugliest damn things I have EVER seen. "What happens when EVA-01 and E.T. have an illegitimate love child?" The Eva Monkey wrote: By expert, he means he is quite adept at drawing them, and he is.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 27, 2005 12:17 am Reichu wrote: If I'm known in Japan, this is the first I've heard of it... O_O

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 27, 2005 12:23 am The Eva Monkey wrote: If I recall correctly, someone from ANF (Wigs, Sharp,???) reported from their time in Japan an encounter with someone who spoke of a website of naked Evas. Its not certain, but an uncanny similiarity to your Eva art page.

[]:             Quote Post by Trotsky » Thu Jan 27, 2005 12:25 am Reichu wrote: That picture is one of the fugliest damn things I have EVER seen.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 27, 2005 12:57 am Trotsky wrote: From the person that spends time drawing monsters in lingerie and lesbian sexual positions. Laugh!

[]:             Quote Post by Man Chong » Thu Jan 27, 2005 1:32 am On an aside, I always find it funny how people seem incapable of distinguishing between erotica and pornography. But nevermind.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Jan 27, 2005 1:39 am Again Alex de la Iglesia: Naked lesbian Evas look for apartment to perform the "scissor"

[]:             Quote Post by Trotsky » Thu Jan 27, 2005 1:52 am Reichu wrote: They happen to be very sexy monsters, I might add. If you are going to draw naked Evas, you might as well let them express their sexuality. Especially if you are writing a story in which they, in fact, DO.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 27, 2005 3:12 am Trotsky wrote: That's the most disturbing thing I've heard all year. No joke.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Jan 27, 2005 4:15 am Cue "Raptor Waltz". Anyway, comething about the Eva logo in that E.T. penis-head-thingamajig looks a little off. If it's official, one wonders why the only time any of us have ever seen it was courtesy of a scan.
